title: Packed-to-unpacked port assignment gives invalid operand compiler errors
body:
Verilator 4.032 2020-04-04 rev v4.030-50-gd3797ade9

`clang++  -I.  -MMD -I/usr/local/Cellar/verilator/4.032/share/verilator/include -I/usr/local/Cellar/verilator/4.032/share/verilator/include/vltstd -DVM_COVERAGE=0 -DVM_SC=0 -DVM_TRACE=1 -faligned-new -fbracket-depth=4096 -Qunused-arguments -Wno-parentheses-equality -Wno-sign-compare -Wno-uninitialized -Wno-unused-parameter -Wno-unused-variable -Wno-shadow     -Wall -O3 --std=c++17   -c -o Vfft__ALLcls.o Vfft__ALLcls.cpp
eIn file included from Vfft__ALLcls.cpp:3:
./Vfft.cpp:1344:58: error: invalid operands to binary expression ('IData *' (aka 'unsigned int *') and 'unsigned int')
                                                         >> 7U) 
                                                         ^  ~~
./Vfft.cpp:1351:81: error: invalid operands to binary expression ('IData *' (aka 'unsigned int *') and 'unsigned int')
                                                                                >> 7U)))))))) 
                                                                                ^  ~~
./Vfft.cpp:1363:58: error: invalid operands to binary expression ('IData *' (aka 'unsigned int *') and 'unsigned int')
                                                         >> 7U) 
                                                         ^  ~~
./Vfft.cpp:1370:81: error: invalid operands to binary expression ('IData *' (aka 'unsigned int *') and 'unsigned int')
                                                                                >> 7U)))))))) 
                                                                                ^  ~~
4 errors generated.
make[1]: *** [Vfft__ALLcls.o] Error 1
make: *** [obj_dir/Vfft] Error 2
`

Attached is my project (which isnt the finest code, and I am still debugging it, but I woul expect it to compile).

My most recent change to to project was to instantiale inst_rndoI and inst_rndoQ in fft.v (the top level module). Without those two, the code works correctly.

I am wondering if my verilog is malformed and verilator did not catch it, or if the tool just had a hard time interpretting what I was trying to do?
[rtl.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/4539704/rtl.zip)

I can work to make a minumum working example aswell (although the above is not one)
